name: Josh Shapiro
bio: Incumbent Governor focusing on bipartisan infrastructure, police recruitment, and expanding the PA economy.
imageAlt: Josh Shapiro smiling in front of a PA flag

q1.label: Support
q1.quote: We need a permanent solution for SEPTA that gives our largest city the tools to fund its future.

q2.label: Support
q2.quote: To lower costs, we must build more, and we must build where the people and the transit already are.

q4.label: Support
q4.quote: It is past time we put more money in the pockets of working Pennsylvanians.

name: Stacy Garrity
bio: Current State Treasurer and Army veteran running on fiscal transparency and taxpayer rights.
imageAlt: Stacy Garrity posing for a campaign portrait

q1.label: Oppose
q1.quote: Philadelphians shouldn't be taxed more to cover for SEPTA's mismanagement.

q2.label: Oppose
q2.quote: One-size-fits-all zoning from Harrisburg or Washington destroys the character of our neighborhoods.

q4.label: Oppose
q4.quote: A forced wage hike will shutter small businesses across the Commonwealth.

q5.label: Oppose
q5.quote: Ranked-choice voting is a confusing scheme that undermines faith in our simple 'one person, one vote' system.

name: Brendan Boyle
bio: Incumbent and Ranking Member of the House Budget Committee, focused on labor rights and transit infrastructure.
imageAlt: Brendan Boyle in front of the Capitol

q1.label: Support
q1.quote: SEPTA is the lifeblood of our city; we must empower it through federal and local support.

q2.label: Support
q2.quote: We face a severe housing shortage; density near transit is a generational necessity.

q3.label: Support
q3.quote: Protecting and expanding the ACA is how we reach every neighbor.

q4.label: Support
q4.quote: The minimum wage is a poverty wage. We need $15 now.

q6.label: Neutral
q6.quote: I support comprehensive reform that is humane but maintains safety.

q7.label: Support
q7.quote: Debt relief is the stimulus working families in Philly need.

q8.label: Neutral
q8.quote: My priority is strengthening the ACA as the quickest path to universal coverage.

name: Jessica Arriaga
bio: Community health coordinator and ward leader running on safer streets and economic opportunity.

q1.label: Oppose
q1.quote: No more blank checks for mismanaged agencies.

q2.label: Oppose
q2.quote: Communities deserve a voice in their own growth, not top-down mandates.

q3.label: Neutral
q3.quote: Care should be community-focused, not bureaucrat-focused.

q4.label: Oppose
q4.quote: Government mandates shouldn't tell small businesses what they can afford.

q6.label: Oppose
q6.quote: We must enforce our laws and protect our borders to keep Philly safe.

q7.label: Oppose
q7.quote: Canceling debt is unfair to those who paid theirs or didn't go to college.

q8.label: Oppose
q8.quote: Government-run healthcare would be a disaster for quality and cost.

name: Salem Snow
bio: Progressive challenger focused on corporate accountability, climate action, and Medicaid expansion.

q1.label: Support
q1.quote: Public transit is a right. Fund it fully and make it free.

q2.label: Support
q2.quote: We need density near transit to stop the housing crisis.

q3.label: Support
q3.quote: Move funding to the people, not the health insurance giants.

q4.label: Support
q4.quote: $15 is the floor. We are fighting for a living wage for all.

q6.label: Support
q6.quote: ICE has failed. We need a humane system that welcomes people.

q7.label: Support
q7.quote: Cancel all student debt and make college a public good.

q8.label: Support
q8.quote: Universal, single-payer healthcare is the only moral option.

name: Dr. Ala Stanford
bio: Physician and founder of the Black Doctors Consortium, focusing on health equity and federal accountability.
imageAlt: Dr. Ala Stanford in medical attire

q1.label: Support
q1.quote: Transit is a social determinant of health. We must empower the city to keep its buses running.

q2.label: Support
q2.quote: We need more housing near our transit hubs to ensure every family has a place to live and a way to work.

q3.label: Support
q3.quote: Equity isn't just a word; it’s a doctor visiting you where you live.

q4.label: Support
q4.quote: No one working full time in this city should live in poverty.

q6.label: Neutral
q6.quote: We need a system that is humane, but I am focused on the health of our citizens first.

q7.label: Support
q7.quote: Canceling debt is about giving our young people the financial health to start families and buy homes.

q8.label: Support
q8.quote: Healthcare must be a human right, guaranteed to every resident without a bill.

name: Chris Rabb
bio: State Representative and professor championing radical democracy reform and restorative justice.
imageAlt: Chris Rabb speaking at a community rally

q1.label: Support
q1.quote: Direct taxing authority for Philly is a matter of municipal sovereignty and survival.

q2.label: Neutral
q2.quote: Density is vital, but we must protect against displacement and respect neighborhood input.

q3.label: Support
q3.quote: Community-led health is the only way to dismantle systemic neglect.

q4.label: Support
q4.quote: A $15 minimum wage is the absolute floor for human dignity.

q6.label: Support
q6.quote: ICE as it exists is a tool of trauma, not safety. We must start over.

q7.label: Support
q7.quote: Free our generation from the shackles of predatory student debt.

q8.label: Support
q8.quote: It's time for a single-payer system that puts people over profits.

name: Morgan Cephas
bio: PA House Delegation chair focused on millennial message and maternal health results.
imageAlt: Morgan Cephas speaking at a maternal health event

q1.label: Support
q1.quote: Fixing SEPTA is my top priority for the city’s economic mobility.

q2.label: Support
q2.quote: Transit-oriented development is the key to sustainable, affordable growth in our city.

q3.label: Support
q3.quote: Community-led health is the only way to dismantle systemic neglect.

q4.label: Support
q4.quote: Philadelphia workers have waited too long for a raise Harrisburg refuses to give.

q7.label: Support
q7.quote: Debt relief is an essential tool for building middle-class wealth in our community.

name: Sharif Street
bio: State Senator and former PA Democratic Party Chair, prioritizing economic opportunity and cannabis reform.
imageAlt: Sharif Street at a community event

q1.label: Neutral
q1.quote: I am open to all options to fund transit, but we must ensure accountability from SEPTA first.

q2.label: Support
q2.quote: We have to incentivize building near transit to tackle the affordability crisis.

q3.label: Support
q3.quote: Empowering community clinics is essential to reaching the underserved.

q4.label: Support
q4.quote: Pennsylvania's minimum wage is a state embarrassment.

q6.label: Support
q6.quote: We need to fix our immigration system so it reflects our values as a city of neighborhoods.

q7.label: Support
q7.quote: Student debt shouldn't be a life sentence.

q8.label: Neutral
q8.quote: I support moving toward universal coverage, exploring all viable paths.

name: Cole Carter
bio: Anti-establishment progressive advocate for $20 minimum wage and tuition-free college.
imageAlt: Cole Carter in a casual community setting

q1.label: Support
q1.quote: Make SEPTA free and fund it by taxing the rich.

q2.label: Oppose
q2.quote: Overriding local zoning is a gift to developers and a curse to our neighborhoods.

q3.label: Support
q3.quote: Take the profit out of healthcare and put the power in the community.

q4.label: Support
q4.quote: $15 was the goal a decade ago; we need a $20 living wage now.

q7.label: Support
q7.quote: Cancel all student debt. Education is a right, not a debt trap.

q8.label: Support
q8.quote: Medicare for All is the only moral choice for this country.
